predictions.reasoning: Diary 'Family issues' is a direct NA signal, consistent with his known stressor (youngest son's attitude, holiday family tension). Behavioral data strongly corroborates: screen use is Z=+41.40 above his afternoon baseline (avoidance/distraction coping), walking is Z=+3.99 above baseline (agitation/restlessness), heavy social app use (3h43m). Drove 1h24m suggests a family visit that generated conflict. Device offline in 4hrs before EMA limits hourly granularity. No similar past days available for calibration (first EMA entry). Profile (GAD-7=12, low extraversion, trait NA=20) amplifies impact of family stressors. PA suppressed below trait level; NA elevated above. ER_desire elevated given distress + behavioral avoidance pattern. worried=TRUE (anxiety trait + family stress), forecasting=TRUE (anxiety + unresolved family conflict). interactions_quality=FALSE (family issues implies poor quality). Confidence limited by lack of comparable past days and sparse hourly data.

Diary 'My family' in the evening follows an earlier 'Family issues' entry (ER_desire=6, receptive). The softened framing suggests partial emotional processing — warm reflection on family during pre-Christmas period, but residual concern from earlier family tensions. Behavioral evidence is fully consistent: 12h screen time, 3h43m social apps, 3220 keyboard chars point to active family communication throughout the day, an unusually high-engagement day vs baseline. Profile (GAD-7=12, PHQ-8=8) sets baseline moderate anxiety/depression. Predicting mixed state: moderate positive from holiday family connection (PA=15), moderate negative from persistent family stress and background anxiety (NA=10). ER_desire reduced slightly from 14:08 level as evening brings some resolution. Still receptive to intervention given ongoing family preoccupation.

Diary 'New day' is an intentional reset framing after yesterday's 'Family issues' (ER_desire=5-6). Morning EMA, neutral-positive tone with no explicit distress. Holiday period (Dec 22) adds mild positive valence but family tension context remains. Behavioral profile matches his 3-day norm: very high screen time (~12h, heavy social apps) is stable pattern for this person, not anomalous. Low mobility consistent with his recent days. Chronic anxiety (GAD-7=12) keeps worried=true as background state. ER_desire consistent with prior two EMAs (5-6). INT_availability=yes follows from the stable pattern and willingness to engage.

Diary 'Youngest son' signals family-related emotional complexity on Dec 22 holiday. Baseline profile identifies youngest son as a known stressor; prior day had 'Family issues'. Despite this tension, holiday context + family presence (driving 2h, elevated walking, heavy social app use 3h54m, high keyboard output) suggests active family engagement rather than withdrawal. ER_desire trend (6→5→7→6) and consistent INT_availability=yes across all prior EMAs today support continued receptivity. PHQ-8=8/GAD-7=12 baseline sustains moderate NA elevation. Most similar past day (Dec 21) had ER_desire=5, available=yes — anchors prediction toward moderate ER with availability.

Diary expresses warm holiday nostalgia ('always loved the night no matter what') — positive affect with implicit resilience. Screen time is at historical top 1% (Z=+42.3), consistent with holiday home-staying and device use. Heavy social/communication app usage (3.5h) aligns with Christmas-season social engagement. Behavioral profile nearly identical to prior two days (similarity 0.96-0.99), both of which showed receptivity (ER=5-7, available=yes). Despite moderate anxiety trait (GAD=12), holiday context and family-centered diary signal positive state dominating. ER_desire estimated at 6 (slight decrease from morning EMAs at 7 due to more positive diary tone). Confident in receptivity given 100% base rate in similar days.

Diary 'Youngest son' mirrors yesterday afternoon entry (ER_desire=7), a known stress source per baseline history. Morning was positive/nostalgic (Xmas Eve). Afternoon behavior shows extreme screen engagement (z=+41 vs personal baseline) and 3.5h social/communication apps with 8267 keystrokes — consistent with active family communication around Christmas, possibly managing son dynamics. Trait GAD-7=12 amplifies worry. Mixed emotional state: holiday warmth elevates PA, youngest son preoccupation sustains NA/worry. Afternoon EMAs have been 100% receptive historically; same 'Youngest son' pattern yesterday yielded ER_desire=7, estimated ~6 today given morning's more positive mood buffer.

Diary 'It's Xmas eve I love Xmas eve' is an unambiguous positive signal — peak holiday anticipation and joy. Behavioral data shows typical indoor sedentary holiday pattern, screen use consistent with prior 3 days (not anomalous), prominent social app use (3h 38m) suggesting family digital connection. No negative stressors in diary, no pain/worry signals. Trait anxiety (GAD-7=12) present but not activated today. ER_desire estimated at 4, matching Dec 23 morning pattern when he expressed similar Christmas Eve anticipation. INT_availability='yes' consistent with 100% session history. Forecasting=true as he's actively anticipating holiday events.

Diary 'Son selfish behavior' is a clear, direct negative emotional signal — interpersonal frustration/anger with youngest son on Christmas Eve, contrasting sharply with the joyful morning entry. This recurring son-related theme (Dec 22, 23, 24 afternoon) represents an escalating stressor. Extreme screen use (Z=+43.85, 767 min vs baseline ~32 min) is consistent with emotional distraction/venting behavior during a conflict. High keyboard activity (5074 chars) further supports active messaging/venting. All 3 behaviorally similar days (Dec 22, 23, 21) showed ER_desire 5-7 with full receptivity when son/family frustration was the theme. Profile (GAD-7=12, Trait NA=20) amplifies negative affect response to interpersonal stressors. Predicting elevated NA (frustrated, sad, worried, lonely), reduced PA, high ER desire, and receptive to intervention.

Christmas morning diary ('It's Xmas') is brief and calm — less expressive than Christmas Eve ('I love Xmas eve') but contextually positive for a family-oriented cancer survivor who cherishes the holidays. Behavioral profile shows extreme screen use (Z=+38.64, top 1%) driven by holiday social app engagement (3h 9m social), consistent with family communication on Christmas Day. Motion is normal (stationary at home). The analogous Christmas Eve morning had ER_desire=1 (lowest in record) with a positive holiday diary, pulling ER_desire estimate down despite similar behavioral days averaging ER=7. Background worry persists (GAD-7=12, son tension from yesterday's ER=9 entry), but morning Christmas framing suppresses NA. INT_availability is 'yes' across all 8 prior observations with no exceptions.

Christmas Day afternoon: diary signals pure positive affect (cooking holiday dinner for family, no stressors mentioned). Behavioral profile supports this — stationary/indoor consistent with home cooking, dominant social app use (3h 9m) + high keyboard activity (3929 chars) reflect holiday communication with family/friends. Screen use elevated above baseline but contextually appropriate for holiday. No youngest-son tension appears today unlike prior entries. Morning EMA showed ER_desire=2 (low, not receptive on positive Xmas morning); afternoon is still holiday-positive but afternoon EMAs tend higher for this person. Similar behavioral days (ER=5,9,7) were more conflicted in diary content — today's diary is lighter, so ER_desire estimated below those analogues at 4. PA elevated (holiday, purposeful activity, family connection); NA suppressed (no active stressors). All session-history entries show INT_availability=yes.

Diary 'Health worries' on Christmas evening signals direct negative affect — anxious rumination after a day of family celebration. Cancer survivor (GAD-7=12) with upcoming CT scan; evening health anxiety is consistent with his baseline pattern. Screen use is severely elevated (Z=+239.61, 770 unlocks) indicating anxious phone-checking rather than celebration. Social apps dominated the day (family Christmas contact), explaining earlier positive affect, but by evening health concerns dominate. All 4 behaviorally-similar days had elevated ER_desire (5-9) and were receptive. Forecasting likely true given cancer-health anticipation. PA diminished but not absent given Christmas family context.

Diary 'Chicago visit' signals a positive post-Christmas social event consistent with this participant's documented pattern of valuing holiday family gatherings. Morning EMA timing is key: his three prior positive-diary mornings (Xmas eve, Xmas day) produced ER_desire=1 and 2 respectively, placing this 'Chicago visit' entry at ~3. The 3h22m driving corroborates travel to/from Chicago. Heavy social app use (1h33m) fits social engagement context. Behaviorally similar days showed receptive=YES (4/4), and this participant has never recorded INT_availability=no. Background anxiety (GAD-7=12) keeps PANAS_Neg slightly elevated but the positive travel context suppresses acute negative affect. Forecasting=true because the visit implies anticipation of (or reflection on) a social event.

Diary clearly signals peak positive affect: successful travel completion to visit granddaughter and great-grandson on Dec 26. The 3h22m driving validates the Chicago trip. Extremely elevated screen (z=+27) and walking (z=+5.9) confirm this is an exceptional behavioral day driven by purposeful travel, not distress. Session memory pattern: ER_desire is low (1-2) during genuine positive family moments, high (7-9) only for stressors (son's behavior, health worries). Morning same-day EMA already showed INT_availability=no during travel phase. Arrival with family now engaged = continued unavailability. Similar-days algorithm misleadingly returns stressful unusual days; diary-first override applies here.

Diary 'Seeing family & friends' is unambiguously positive — participant arrived in Chicago that afternoon (ER_desire dropped to 1 upon arrival) and is now in the social/emotional peak of a holiday family visit. Behavioral data confirms: 3h22m driving (travel), 74 min walking (+20 SD above baseline — active with family), heavy social app use. Within-day ER_desire trajectory (4 → 1 → ~2) signals progressive emotional relief from family contact. Similar-day algorithm matched holiday-activity-pattern days with negative diary content, but that is a behavioral confound; the within-day signal and diary clearly override. Anxiety is low in present-moment positive social context despite trait GAD-7=12. Participant reliably reports availability=yes when settled.

Day 2 of Chicago family visit — 'A family visit to Chicago' continues yesterday's positive trajectory (ER=4,1,1 on 12/26). Social apps dominated screen use (2h46m), consistent with documenting/sharing family moments. Screen time very elevated vs baseline (Z=+28.41), indicating high engagement rather than distress. Similar-day precedent: 12/26 'Seeing family & friends' yielded ER=1. Participant's baseline profile identifies family visits as peak positive state. Underlying GAD-7=12 anxiety present but not surfacing today. Low ER_desire (3) expected based on 12/26 morning (ER=4) and the continued positive family context.

Diary is unambiguously positive — visiting grandchildren and friends in Chicago, a deeply meaningful social event for this 68-year-old cancer survivor. Strong cross-modal consistency: 2h46m social app usage, 695 screen opens, 8h screen time all validate active social engagement. Session memory shows all 3 prior Chicago EMAs had ER_desire=1, including the morning EMA on this same day. Similar-days algorithm matched high-ER 'youngest son' days on behavioral features alone — overridden by diary content and within-trip pattern. Elevated screen time (z=+28.65) reflects social sharing/communication, not stress. Predicting peak positive affect, minimal negative affect, near-zero ER desire consistent with the established Chicago-visit pattern.

Evening EMA closes out a uniformly positive Chicago family visit day. Diary 'Holidays & family' continues the same theme as the morning ('A family visit to Chicago') and afternoon ('My visit with grand kids & friends in Chicago') EMAs, both of which showed ER_desire=1. Behavioral narrative is consistent: high social app usage (2h 46m), above-baseline walking (22 min, z=+5.36), indoor environment — all fitting an active holiday gathering. Screen usage is extremely elevated vs. baseline but aligns with holiday browsing pattern seen across Dec 26-27. The son-related tension from pre-trip (Dec 22-24) is absent. Background anxiety trait (GAD-7=12) suppresses NA slightly above floor. ER_desire predicted at 1 — consistent with 5 consecutive low-ER readings during this trip.

Diary 'Good night's sleep' signals calm, content morning with no active stressors. Morning EMA in the Chicago family-visit window (Dec 26-27 both had ER_desire=1 with brief positive diary entries). Screen activity is extremely elevated vs. morning baseline but explained by social/entertainment use during family visit. Walking slightly above norm. No health worries or family conflict mentioned. Anxiety trait (GAD-7=12) present but dormant this morning. Pattern strongly suggests low ER desire and positive affect above personal mean, consistent with recent holiday/family context.

Diary 'Family attitudes' (afternoon, after positive morning 'Good night's sleep') matches a consistent pattern: all prior negative-family afternoon entries (Family issues ER=6, Youngest son ER=7×2, Son selfish behavior ER=9) had INT_availability=yes and ER_desire 6-9. Baseline summary confirms son's attitudes are a recurring stressor. Behavioral data strongly corroborates: screen use is top 1% (z=+39.91), social apps dominate at 3h41m, and walking is elevated (z=+3.54) — classic distraction-seeking and restlessness pattern. Cross-modal consistency is high. ER_desire estimated at 7, NA elevated, PA suppressed below personal mean. All historical afternoon negative-family entries show INT_availability=yes.

Diary 'Family behaviors' continues the afternoon tension theme (prior EMA ER=9, 'Family attitudes'). Participant returned from positive Chicago family visit (Dec 26-27, ER=1 throughout) and has re-encountered the family dynamic stressor that recurs around his youngest son and family tensions since Dec 21. Screen use is extreme (z=+48 vs baseline, 10h53m total, social apps 3h41m dominant), consistent with passive distraction/rumination post-conflict. Walking and driving reflect travel home. GAD-7=12 and recurring family-tension diary pattern support elevated worry and negative affect. Afternoon ER=9 anchors high ER_desire; slight evening decay modeled as ER=7. INT_availability=yes matches this person's near-universal pattern.

Diary 'New Day' signals fresh-start mentality with no overt stressors. Day follows positive Chicago family visit (Dec 26-27); previous evening ER_desire=1 (low distress). Behavioral profile is anomalously active: walking +5.65 SD above baseline, screen use far elevated (563 opens, 9.5h), significant driving (2h12m) and social app use (2.5h), suggesting engaged and outgoing state. GAD-7=12 contributes baseline anxiety but morning neutral diary + low prior-evening ER_desire argues against elevated NA. Moderate PA expected from post-family-visit carry-over warmth and fresh morning framing.

Evening diary 'My family' mirrors the identical 12-21 evening entry (ER_desire=5, receptive). Post-Chicago holiday visit context; morning calm ('New Day', ER_desire=2). High social app usage (2h 25m) consistent with active family communication. Screen time extremely elevated vs personal baseline (Z=+40.94) suggesting active engagement. No acute stressor signaled — reflective, warm family thoughts dominate. Moderate anxiety baseline (GAD-7=12) keeps worried=true. ER_desire ~4 slightly above personal mean of 3.8. Similar days pattern: 3/5 were receptive, INT_availability=yes.

Diary 'Almost a new year' is brief and contemplative — neutral forward-looking reflection, no stressors. Consistent with the strong morning-EMA pattern: ER_desire has been 1-2 for all mornings since Dec 24 after holiday travel. Behavioral narrative shows high social screen use (typical holiday-at-home baseline), mild walking above norm, indoor/stationary. find_similar_days returned high-ER afternoons with distress diaries — behavioral overlap but emotional context mismatch. Forecasting=true given the new year contemplation. Low affect intensity overall; INT_availability=yes consistent with all prior mornings.

Diary 'Calmness at my house' is the clearest positive-calm signal in this participant's session history. Morning EMA (same day, ER=1, 'Almost a new year') established a tranquil baseline. Behavioral data fully supports: stationary home day, indoor, leisure screen consumption (social/entertainment apps). No family conflict language, no health worry language. Historical pattern: low ER_desire consistently pairs with benign diary entries (holidays, family visits, rest), while high ER_desire pairs with conflict language — today is firmly the former. Find_similar_days returned high-ER days that share behavioral profile but diverge on diary content; diary signal overrides. INT_availability set to 'yes' per strong base rate (23/23 days except one travel day). Confidence reduced slightly due to find_similar_days ambiguity and the participant's moderate background anxiety (GAD-7=12).

Diary 'Interaction with family' follows two calm low-ER EMAs today (ER=1 both). Full-day arc is consistently calm. Holiday-season family interaction is this person's primary positive emotional anchor per baseline summary. Behavioral pattern (high screen/social apps, driving, indoor) aligns with active family engagement. Post-Chicago-trip trend shows ER stabilized at 1-2 since Dec 28. Find_similar_days flagged stressful family days (son conflict) but semantic context today is opposite — no conflict language, no worry keywords. Predicting low NA, moderate PA, ER_desire=1 consistent with today's established pattern.

Morning NYE entry 'Almost a new year >2024' is nearly identical to 12/30 morning (ER_desire=1, receptive=no). Forward-looking anticipation, no stressors expressed. Behavioral pattern: elevated screen time consistent with recent holiday baseline, slightly elevated walking (z=+4.26 above past week avg), heavy social app use (4h 44m) on NYE — all coherent with calm holiday engagement. Individual pattern: morning EMAs with positive/neutral diary reliably produce ER_desire=1-2, INT_availability=yes but not receptive. GAD-7 baseline anxiety present but no explicit health or family triggers today. Cancer scan anxiety doesn't appear until January per baseline history.

Diary 'Anticipation for NYEve at home' is unambiguously positive — holiday excitement consistent with documented love of Christmas/holidays. Session history shows ER_desire invariably drops to 0-2 on positive holiday days (only spikes 7-9 on family conflict days). Morning EMA today was ER_desire=1 confirming calm start. Recent 5-day streak: ER_desire 0-2 with 8% overall receptivity. Behavior aligns: heavy social media browsing (4h 44m), indoor, minimal outgoing communication — quiet NYE preparation at home. No family conflict signals, no health worry language. Predict low ER_desire (~1), positive affect with anticipation/cheerfulness/gratitude, no negative states. INT_availability=yes is near-constant for this person.

New Year's Day morning EMA. Diary 'New Year' is a terse holiday marker consistent with this person's pattern on positive occasions (cf. 'It's Xmas,' 'Anticipation for NYEve at home'). Most similar day is 2023-12-31 (sim=0.98, ER_desire=1). Heavy screen time (+55SD above norm) reflects holiday social media consumption, not distress — social apps dominated (4.5h). Fully sedentary indoor day expected for introverted (E=10) male. No family conflict or health anxiety signals in diary. Background anxiety (GAD-7=12) present but CT scan not yet salient. Strong prior: 4/5 most similar days have ER_desire=1, INT_availability=yes.

New Year's Day diary is optimistic and forward-looking ('new horizons, new starts') with no negative themes. Behavioral profile shows a quiet holiday at home: highly sedentary (19 min walking), very high screen/social app usage (4.5h social apps) consistent with digital socializing on the holiday. Most similar past day is NYE (sim=0.98) which yielded ER_desire=1, non-receptive — an identical holiday-at-home pattern. Morning EMA was also ER=1. Established pattern for this participant: celebratory/positive days → PANAS_Pos elevated, PANAS_Neg suppressed, ER_desire ~1-2. Trait anxiety (GAD-7=12) provides mild background worry potential but diary shows no activation today. Forecasting=true due to explicit new-year aspiration language.

Diary 'New Year New Beginnings' is brief but positive — directly mirrors Jan 1 entries (ER=1, not receptive). Top-2 similar days (similarity 0.99-1.00) are Dec 31 and Jan 1 morning, both ER_desire=1, not receptive. Pattern is highly consistent: morning EMA + positive diary → ER_desire=1, INT_availability=yes but not receptive. High screen time (social apps) on a holiday is normal for this person. No health worries mentioned. Forecasting=true given New Year forward-looking theme.

Diary 'New beginnings' is the afternoon continuation of morning 'New Year New Beginnings' (ER_desire=0). Two-day streak of identical new-year-optimism with zero stressors. Most similar days (Jan 1, Dec 31) both show ER_desire=1, available=yes, receptive=no — strong convergent signal. High social app use (3h) reflects passive browsing consistent with calm indoor day; zero comm use suggests content solitude. Baseline GAD-7=12 adds mild NA floor but no acute anxiety triggers evident. CT scan worry (Jan 22) not yet prominent. All signals align: low ER_desire, available but not receptive, moderate positive affect.

Diary 'Youngest son attitude & disrespect for me' is the most explicit son-conflict entry in this study period. Historical pattern: all diary entries explicitly mentioning youngest son/selfish behavior map to ER_desire=7-9 and INT_availability=yes. Today shows a strong within-day shift: morning/afternoon entries were positive (New Year optimism, ER=0-1), indicating conflict emerged in the afternoon-evening. Behavioral data supports emotional disturbance: extremely high screen time (13h, 708 opens) with heavy social app use but zero communication app use suggests distraction-seeking rather than support-seeking coping — consistent with agitated rumination. Sedentary indoor pattern (16h stationary) aligns with emotional withdrawal post-conflict. Find-similar-days returns low-ER holiday days, but diary sentiment completely overrides behavioral clustering. Profile context (mild depression, moderate anxiety, Trait NA=20) amplifies expected NA response to perceived family disrespect. Predicting elevated NA with clear hurt/miserable/worried affect, suppressed PA, high ER desire, and continued intervention availability.

Diary 'It's my birthday today' is a clear positive signal for a 68-year-old cancer survivor who deeply values family. Birthday morning brings warm anticipation, happiness, and gratitude (surviving cancer to see another birthday). The behavioral narrative strongly validates this: extreme screen activity (z=+35.65), high social app use (2h 2m), and 10163 keyboard chars are all consistent with receiving and responding to birthday messages/calls. The 4h 38m driving suggests a birthday outing later in the day. No pre-EMA device data (device off 21:20–09:20) so prediction relies on diary + profile context. His morning EMA pattern consistently shows ER_desire 0-2 and INT_availability=yes even on holidays, so that pattern holds. Background GAD-7=12 anxiety persists but is dampened by birthday positivity. Predicting moderate-elevated PA, suppressed NA, low ER desire consistent with his calm morning baseline.

Birthday day for participant 119. Diary 'Today my bdy' (birthday), confirmed by morning EMA. Behavioral evidence strongly consistent: screen time at extreme outlier (Z=+37.67, 10h7m vs. typical 29min), heavy social app use (2h2m), 4h38m driving outing, elevated walking (Z=+4.95), 10k keyboard chars — all pointing to birthday social engagement (receiving/responding to messages, going out). Yesterday evening son-related stress (ER_desire=7) appears to have resolved overnight; morning EMA was ER_desire=2. Predicting elevated PA, suppressed NA for birthday context. Background health anxiety (GAD-7=12, upcoming CT Jan 22) likely present but muted by birthday positivity. INT_availability=yes is invariant for this participant across all 34 prior observations.

Birthday celebration with son and hubby (cake and presents) — strongest positive family event in the diary record. Same-day EMAs at 09:20 and 14:12 show ER_desire trending 2→1, establishing clear momentum into a low-ER evening. Historical pattern for this participant is consistent: positive family days produce ER_desire 0-2 and non-receptive status, while conflict days spike to 7-9. Behavioral data corroborates: 4h38m driving suggests an outing (birthday dinner/activity), elevated social app usage aligns with receiving birthday messages. No health concerns, no family conflict signals today. GAD-7=12 baseline anxiety partially suppressed by celebratory context.

Diary signals a spontaneous positive mood shift ('different mindset... feels good all things considered'), reinforced by birthday celebration the prior day and New Year optimism. Morning EMA with positive/neutral diary content consistently yields ER_desire 0-2 in this participant's history; background stressors (son's attitude, upcoming CT scan) acknowledged but not active today. Behavioral data supports: elevated physical activity (walking 138 min vs baseline 5.4 min average), high social app use and keyboard activity indicate social engagement rather than withdrawal. Similar days with mixed ER_desire but diary content decisively tips toward low desire to regulate. Background trait anxiety (GAD-7=12) keeps NA above zero but diary explicitly states feeling good.

Diary reveals strong positive anticipation: family arriving for a visit is the dominant signal, while 'my body' reflects mild health awareness typical of this cancer survivor rather than acute anxiety. Behavioral data powerfully validates this: walking 138 min (Z=+14.6, top 1%) and escalating driving throughout the day (20→66→86 min across morning/afternoon/evening) is the behavioral fingerprint of someone actively preparing for and receiving family visitors. High keyboard activity and social app use are consistent with coordination. Morning EMA (ER=1, 'feels good') establishes the baseline mood for the day. Historical pattern is clear: this person's ER spikes only during youngest son conflict or acute health worry—neither is present today. The new year framing and family arrival anticipation strongly predict elevated PA and low NA. CT scan anxiety (Jan 22) has not yet materialized.

Diary signals clear positive anticipation for son-and-grandsons family visit — the strongest positive diary theme in this participant's history. Pattern from 35+ prior observations shows ER_desire=1-2 on positive family days vs. ER_desire=7-9 on youngest-son-conflict days. Behavioral data confirms: elevated walking (+2.74 SD), driving 1h25m, and 2h+ social app use all consistent with active preparation and family coordination. No health anxiety or interpersonal stressors detected. Trait anxiety (GAD-7=12) provides a low-level NA floor, but today's context strongly suppresses it.

Diary is unambiguous: 'Anticipation of a family coming to visit' signals clear positive affect. Consistent with morning EMA diary ('Son & family coming for visit with grandsons') and Jan 4 diary ('family coming for a visit'). Family visits are the strongest positive emotional driver in this participant's history (Chicago trip, Christmas). No mention of youngest son conflict or health worries today. Behavioral signature aligns: elevated walking (Z=+4.32 vs afternoon baseline) and 85 min driving consistent with visit preparation; 2h 13m social apps consistent with family coordination. Screen use (825 min) is this participant's norm. Morning ER_desire=0 anchors afternoon prediction at low end. Historical pattern shows receptive=YES only when ER_desire ≥4 with explicit negative trigger — today shows neither.

Diary 'Hotel stay with my visiting grandsons' signals peak positive affect — the culmination of a 3-day anticipated family visit. Behavioral data fully corroborates: extreme screen activity (z=+21.92), elevated walking (z=+4.92), and 2h+ driving all align with active hotel-stay family day. Social app usage (1h 28m) consistent with shared family moments. Session memory confirms this person's positive family days reliably produce low ER_desire (0-2) and high PA. No stressor signals (youngest son, health anxiety) present in diary. Morning EMA timing and uniformly positive cross-modal evidence support confident positive prediction.

Diary 'Family visit & pool time with grandsons' signals peak positive affect — active, joyful engagement with grandchildren. Behavioral evidence strongly corroborates: walking at Z=+7.55 (pool/outdoor activity), elevated social app use and keyboard activity consistent with sharing/communicating during the visit. This follows a 2-day anticipation arc and mirrors prior positive family days (Dec 27, Jan 4) which both showed ER_desire=1, receptive=no. No health anxiety signals today despite background GAD-7=12. Low ER_desire and high PA expected.

Diary 'Family visiting last 3 days' confirms ongoing family visit — grandchildren present (hotel/pool on Jan 6). Session history shows ER_desire=0-1 on all prior family visit days (Dec 26-28 Chicago, Jan 5-6). Behavioral data strongly corroborates: screen_on_min elevated Z=+29.43 with social+comm apps dominating (3h 51m), walking elevated (Z=+2.98), indoor — classic family gathering pattern. No negative signals: no health worries, no son attitude conflict mentioned. Morning EMA during active 3-day family visit. Similar past days (family context) all show ER_desire=1, available=yes. High confidence in positive affect, low NA, low ER_desire.

Diary captures the exact moment of family departure after a positive 3-day visit with son, daughter-in-law, and grandsons. Session history Jan 5-7 shows consistently low ER_desire (0-1) throughout the visit, indicating high positive affect during the stay. The neutral-factual tone of the departure diary suggests bittersweet processing rather than acute distress. Behavioral data strongly aligns: extremely elevated social (2h40m) + communication (1h11m) app usage consistent with staying connected with departing family via phone; elevated walking (Z=+4.64) likely reflects farewell activity. Screen usage massively above baseline (Z=+29.74) driven by the social event context. This person (GAD-7=12, PHQ-8=8, low extraversion=10) typically experiences post-visit loneliness, hence mild NA elevation (sad, lonely) while retaining positive affect from the quality family interaction. ER_desire predicted low (~2) consistent with his week-long pattern of 0-1, and INT_availability shifts to yes now that family has left and he is alone at home.

Diary 'Son attitude & behavior, big change' capped a 3-day family visit where ER_desire held at 0–1 throughout, reversing the Jan-2 'youngest son disrespect' pattern (ER=7). Big change most plausibly = positive behavioral shift observed in son, yielding cautious relief and gratitude. Post-visit bittersweet sadness (family departed afternoon, ER=5 at parting) + chronic anxiety (GAD-7=12, CT scan Jan 22 looming) sustain moderate NA. Evening is 100% stationary, heavy social/comm app use (staying connected with departed family). Screen time extremely elevated vs baseline (Z≈33) consistent with multi-day family-visit pattern. ER_desire_State false — ER=4 above typical (0–2) but below receptive threshold seen historically (≥5–7). Forecasting true: 'big change' implies future-oriented reflection on son's trajectory.

Diary directly signals health anxiety: 'getting sick & hope it's not Covid.' For a 68-year-old cancer survivor with GAD-7=12, illness onset is acutely distressing — baseline profile explicitly notes COVID fears as a recurring theme. Context compounds: family just departed after 3-day visit (possible exposure), yesterday PM already at ER=8 due to son's attitude. Behavioral evidence is consistent with feeling ill: highly stationary (16h 57m), elevated screen use (786 opens, 10h 13m), indoor environment — classic sick-day pattern. Worry, afraid, and pain states flagged by diary; lonely elevated as family just left and person is now sick alone. Forecasting active ('hope it's not Covid' = future health concern). ER desire ~7 consistent with health-fear distress for this person.

Diary explicitly states physical illness with COVID fear — a highly salient health threat for a 68-year-old cancer survivor with GAD-7=12 and an upcoming CT scan. Morning EMA (same theme, ER_desire=6) confirms the distress has persisted into afternoon. Behavioral data provides unambiguous corroboration: stationary time (16h57m, Z=+55.91) and screen time (10h13m, Z=+38.00) are both extreme outliers — top 1% of all his hours — consistent with a sick day confined to bed. Fully indoor, minimal walking. Session memory shows he reliably becomes receptive (INT_availability=yes, receptive=YES) when ER_desire≥5, and his baseline ER_desire is almost always 1-2, making today's elevated distress clearly above personal mean. The combination of physical illness, COVID anxiety, post-family-visit loneliness (son left Jan 7), and cancer survivor health vigilance drives high NA, low PA, and strong ER desire.
